2604.09466.title: Across the Levels of Analysis: Explaining Predictive Processing in Humans Requires More Than Machine-Estimated Probabilities
2604.09466.paper_summary: Under the lens of Marr's levels of analysis, we critique and extend two claims about language models (LMs) and language processing: first, that predicting upcoming linguistic information based on context is central to language processing, and second, that many advances in psycholinguistics would be impossible without large language models (LLMs). We further outline future directions that combine the strengths of LLMs with psycholinguistic models.
2604.09466.paper_summary_zh: 在馬爾的分析層次的視角下，我們批判並擴展了關於語言模型（LMs）和語言處理的兩個主張：首先，基於上下文預測即將到來的語言信息對於語言處理至關重要；其次，許多心理語言學的進展在沒有大型語言模型（LLMs）的情況下將是不可能的。我們進一步概述了未來的方向，結合了LLMs的優勢與心理語言學模型。
2604.09466.author: Sathvik Nair et.al.
